title: glyceria fluitans subsp. obtusiflora
intro: Glyceria fluitans subsp. obtusiflora, also known as the floating sweet grass or soft water manna grass, is a perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the grass family, Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Glyceria fluitans subsp. obtusiflora include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Traditionally, this plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be used in tincture form, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
